title: Manual DNA Extraction Protocol for Campylobacter Detection in Canine and Bovine Feces
author: Sara D. Lawhon
materials: QIAamp PowerFecal Pro DNA Isolation Kit

steps.title: Fecal samples should be stored at...
steps.instruction: Fecal samples should be stored at 4-8 °C, refrigerated until processed.

steps.title: Samples should be processed within 72...
steps.instruction: Samples should be processed within 72 hours of receipt at the laboratory .

steps.title: Use 8 g of stool in 50 mL conical tubes
steps.instruction: Use 8 g of stool in 50 mL conical tubes

steps.title: Add 32 mL 1X PBS Life...
steps.instruction: Add 32 mL 1X PBS Life Technologies Catalog #10010-023 to 8 g of feces. The feces in PBS will hereafter be called the feces-PBS slurry. Making the Feces-PBS slurry.

steps.title: This protocol uses VetMAX Xeno Internal...
steps.instruction: This protocol uses VetMAX Xeno Internal Positive Control ND Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A29762/A29764 as a control for PCR inhibition. Note: This reagent can either be added at step 15 to serve as a DNA extraction control or after DNA extraction is complete to serve as the PCR inhibition control. If the laboratory has a system in place for DNA extraction and PCR inhibition, that method can be substituted in this protocol.

steps.title: Allow samples to sit for 00:02:00...
steps.instruction: Allow samples to sit for 00:02:00 after adding the 32 mL PBS

steps.title: Vortex samples for 00:00:30 to prepare fecal slurry
steps.instruction: Vortex samples for 00:00:30 to prepare fecal slurry

steps.title: Extract DNA from the feces using...
steps.instruction: Extract DNA from the feces using the QIAamp PowerFecal Pro DNA Isolation Kit Qiagen Catalog #51804-50 following the manufacturer's protocol (outlined below). Tip: If you are following the manufacturer’s printed protocol, whenever the text says “vortex briefly” vortex for at least 5-10 seconds.

steps.title: Plates and enrichment broth should be...
steps.instruction: Plates and enrichment broth should be incubated in a microaerophilic environment at 42 °C, + or - 2 .

steps.title: Remaining samples from the associated culture...
steps.instruction: Remaining samples from the associated culture method can be used for molecular detection.

steps.title: Check solution CD3
steps.instruction: Check solution CD3. If solution CD3 has precipitated, heat solution to 60 °C in a water bath until precipitate has dissolved.

steps.title: To a microcentrifuge tube, add 1...
steps.instruction: To a microcentrifuge tube, add 1 mL of feces-PBS slurry from Sample Preparation step 7. Pipetting the Feces-PBS slurry to a microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Remove and carefully discard 800 µL...
steps.instruction: Remove and carefully discard 800 µL of the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Removing 800 uL of the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 800 µL Solution CD1 (Qiagen),...
steps.instruction: Add 800 µL Solution CD1 (Qiagen), and add 4 µL VetMAX Xeno Internal Positive Control, and resuspend the fecal pellet. VetMAX Xeno Internal Positive Control ND Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A29762/A29764

steps.title: Secure the bead tubes horizontally on...
steps.instruction: Secure the bead tubes horizontally on a Vortex Adapter (Tube holder, Qiagen (MoBio), 13000-V1-24) Vortexer with Qiagen (MoBio) vortex adapter. or secure the tubes on a flat-bed vortex pad with tape.

steps.title: Vortex at maximum speed for 00:10:00 10 min
steps.instruction: Vortex at maximum speed for 00:10:00 10 min. Tip: If vortexing more than 12 tubes, add an additional 5 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Transfer 500-600 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500-600 µL of the supernatant to a clean 2mL Microcentrifuge Tube. Note: Expect 500-600 µl of supernatant. The supernatant may still contain some stool particles.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Avoiding the pellet, transfer up to...
steps.instruction: Avoiding the pellet, transfer up to 700 µL of supernatant to a clean 2 ml Microcentrifuge Tube Note: Expect 500-600 µl of supernatant.

steps.title: Add 600 µL of Solution CD3 and vortex for 00:00:05
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL of Solution CD3 and vortex for 00:00:05

steps.title: Load 650 µL of lysate onto a MB Spin Column
steps.instruction: Load 650 µL of lysate onto a MB Spin Column. Spin Column

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow-through, place the MB...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through, place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2ml Collection Tube and repeat steps 24 and 25 to ensure that all of the supernatant has been loaded onto the MB Spin Column.

steps.title: Carefully place the MB Spin Column...
steps.instruction: Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2ml Collection Tube. Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Solution EA...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow through and place...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through and place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2 ml Collection Tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Solution C5
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Solution C5.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow through and place...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through and place the MB Spin Column in a clean 2 ml Collection Tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes

steps.title: Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a new 1
steps.instruction: Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a new 1.5 ml Elution Tube

steps.title: Add 60 µL of Solution C6...
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL of Solution C6 to the center of filter membrane

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the MB Spin Column
steps.instruction: Discard the MB Spin Column. The DNA is now ready for PCR.

steps.title: If PCR will be performed within...
steps.instruction: If PCR will be performed within 1-5 days store the DNA at 4 °C . If PCR will be performed after 5 days post DNA isolation, store the DNA at -20 °C until ready to run PCR

steps.title: so detection parameters appropriate for FAM...
steps.instruction: so detection parameters appropriate for FAM or SYBR should be used.The developing laboratory uses Applied Biosystems™ VetMAX™ Xeno™ Internal Positive Control DNA (ThermoFisher Scientific A29762 or A

steps.title: and the VetMAX™ Xeno™ Internal Positive...
steps.instruction: and the VetMAX™ Xeno™ Internal Positive Control - VIC™ Assay (ThermoFisher Scientific A29765 or A

steps.title: to monitor for PCR inhibition
steps.instruction: to monitor for PCR inhibition. Threshold is set at 0.2.

steps.title: Create run on ABI 7500 Fast machine
steps.instruction: Create run on ABI 7500 Fast machine. Setting up the ABI 7500 Fast Thermocycler: Refer to the manual provided for running the machine. Reaction times are as follows in Table 1. 7500 Fast Real-Time PCR System, desktop (Applied Biosystems, 4351107) A B C D E F 1 Stage Function Temperature Duration (sec) Repeats Optics 2 Reverse Transcription 50°C 300 3 Stage 1 Initial PCR Activation 95.0°C 20 Off 4 Stage 2 Annealing 95.0°C 3 Repeats 45 times Off 5 Stage 2 Extension 60°C 60 On Table 1a. PCR amplification conditions in ABI 7500 under standard conditions are as follows: A B C D E F 1 Stage Function Temperature Duration (sec) Repeats Optics 2 Reverse Transcription 50°C 300 3 Stage 1 Initial PCR Activation 95.0°C 20 Off 4 Stage 2 Annealing 95.0°C 3 Repeats 45 times Off 5 Stage 2 Extension 60°C 60 On Table 1b. PCR amplification conditions in ABI 7500 under FAST conditions are as follows: Passive reference dye should be set as ROX

steps.title: and negative ( E
steps.instruction: and negative ( E. coli ATCC

steps.title: control and a “No Template” negative control
steps.instruction: control and a “No Template” negative control. For calculations, reference Table

steps.title: PCR Worksheet for Reagent Volumes for...
steps.instruction: PCR Worksheet for Reagent Volumes for Master Mix Preparation for Campylobacter jejuni PCR. Table2 PCR Worksheet Campy Manual Extraction.xlsx

steps.title: Combine reagents except for the DNA template in a 1
steps.instruction: Combine reagents except for the DNA template in a 1.5ml or 2ml microcentrifuge tube and mix well.

steps.title: Aliquot 18 µL into each PCR...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 18 µL into each PCR tube or reaction well if using a plate.

steps.title: Add samples and positive ( C
steps.instruction: Add samples and positive ( C. jejuni ATCC 33291, ATCC 33292, or ATCC 33560 DNA) and negative ( E. coli ATCC 25922 DNA) controls along with PCR inhibition controls VetMAX Xeno Internal Positive Control ND Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A29762/A29764 ;Lot_________________).

steps.title: Place Samples in thermocycler and perform run
steps.instruction: Place Samples in thermocycler and perform run.

steps.title: Following run, analyze the results
steps.instruction: Following run, analyze the results. Results should be reported with a Ct value and interpretation.

steps.title: Samples with a Ct value of...
steps.instruction: Samples with a Ct value of 40 or less should be interpreted as positive. Samples with a Ct value >40 or an undetermined Ct value should be considered negative. A B C 1 Target CT Value Interpretation 2 gyrA Detected 3 >40 Not detected Table 3.Interpretation of Ct Value A B 1 Oligo Name 5’-3’ Sequence 2 gyrA-F AAGATACGGTCGATTTTGTTCCA 3 gyrA-R CTACAGCTATACCACTTGAACCATTTAATA 4 gyrA-P 5’-[FAM]TGATGGTTCAGAAAGCGAACCTGATGTTTT[BHQ1] 3’ Table 4. Primers and Probe

steps.title: Transfer the fecal pellet that is...
steps.instruction: Transfer the fecal pellet that is resuspended in CD1 to a bead tube. Bead tube from the manufacturer.
